summary:
The cause(s) of apical periodontitis include pulp disease complications as well as physical irritation by restoration defects or other obstructions; thus, multiple etiologies are possible based on different scenarios involving tooth structure integrity issues. To summarize succinctly while capturing essential information for diagnosing causes of apical periodontitis, it includes diverse mechanisms such as pulpal health problems alongside structural anomalies leading to tissue inflammation around roots due to direct trauma or infection spread via dentinal tubules into periapical tissues. 

Final Summary: Multiple factors may lead to apical periodontitis, including pulp infections and physical damage caused by poor restorations or wedge-shaped debris caught between adjacent teeth. Thus, option \( \boxed{D} \), representing an amalgamation of these potential triggers, accurately reflects its broad applicability across varied cases presenting with this condition.
